feat(frontend): add Upstash Redis rate limiting to chat endpoint (#17)

Rate limit frontend chat to 200 messages/day/IP using Upstash Redis.

### Changes

- **`frontend/lib/ratelimit.ts`** — New module wrapping

singleton, gracefully disabled when env vars are absent.

IP extracted from `x-forwarded-for`.

`KV_REST_API_TOKEN`
- **`.env.example`** — Documents new Upstash env vars

### Usage

Set `KV_REST_API_URL` and `KV_REST_API_TOKEN` to enable. Without them,
rate limiting is a no-op.

commit.message:
Replace mean-pooling with last-token selection in Q² quantization (#23)

* docs: remove mean-pooling; argue it increases noise and lowers signal

The design docs incorrectly described mean-pooling token activations as
part of the algorithm. The design position is that mean-pooling as a
dimensional-reduction technique increases noise and lowers signal: the
centroid of token vectors lies inside the unit ball, and the subsequent
L2 renormalisation amplifies model-specific bias rather than the
document's distinctive semantic content.

Changes:
- DESIGN.md §1.1: replace mean-pooling description with single-position
  activation; add explicit "Why not mean-pool?" argument
- DESIGN.md §2.2: remove "mean-pooled" qualifier from activation description
- README.md: drop mean-pool step from algorithm, renumber steps, update
  mermaid diagram, fix q2 dtype note
- TESTING.md: fix activation-source description

https://claude.ai/code/session_018cnKGPGZq7YUJ8RGfAJc48

* fix: remove mean-pooling from kernel and all call sites

The WASM kernel, TypeScript fallback, and all tests were performing
mean-pooling over token positions before L2-normalising, directly
contradicting the design argument that mean-pooling increases noise
and lowers signal (DESIGN.md §1.1).

Changes:
- q2.wat: replace the 3-step zero/accumulate/divide loop with a
  single load of the last token position (seq_len − 1); remove the
  now-unused $s and $seq_len_f locals; update all step numbers and
  comments
- q2.ts: replace meanPoolAndNormalise with l2Normalise (L2-norm only,
  no pooling); update Q2Kernel.quantise and q2EncodeDirect JSDoc;
  update dtype-4 comment
- app.ts: WASM path already delegates to the kernel (which now reads
  the last token); TS fallback now slices the last token row and calls
  l2Normalise instead of meanPoolAndNormalise
- types.ts: update EmbeddingMsg JSDoc to reflect last-token semantics
- test/q2.test.ts: replace meanPoolAndNormalise describe block with
  l2Normalise tests
- test/t0–t4.test.ts: replace all meanPoolAndNormalise(vec, 1, n)
  calls with l2Normalise(vec, n) (seqLen=1 was a no-op for pooling)
- Rebuild WASM binary (920 bytes, down from 1080 — simpler kernel)

All 208 tests pass.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018cnKGPGZq7YUJ8RGfAJc48

commit.message:
fix(whitepaper): correct line coverage framing, remove hallucination language

Line coverage (93.1%) was a real, accurate measurement. The structural
issue is that line coverage measures execution while mutation score
measures detection. A gap between them reveals tests that cover code
without verifying it. When MSI reached 93.10% (matching line coverage),
the gap closed to zero — every covered line verified.

Removed 'hallucinated', 'fabricated', 'aspirational' framing throughout.
Updated all three template changes bullets and §1268 to reflect correct
execution vs. detection framing.

fix: sync `class_names` from dataset after training so `predict()` returns correct labels (#816)

* sync class_names from dataset after training so predict() returns correct labels (#509)

After trainer.fit(), RFDETR.train() now syncs RFDETRDataModule.class_names
back to self.model.class_names so that predict() returns dataset labels
instead of falling through to COCO defaults.

BestModelCallback._save_checkpoint() now enriches TrainConfig with dataset
class names (regular and EMA paths) before saving, so reloaded checkpoints
also carry the correct label mapping.

Five regression tests added covering both the in-memory sync and the
checkpoint serialisation paths.

- Updated `class_names` checks to use `is None` instead of truthiness in multiple paths (e.g., training sync, checkpoint saving) to avoid overwriting empty lists.
- Added regression tests validating that explicit `class_names=[]` are not replaced by dataset defaults (regular and EMA checkpoint paths).
- Use `getattr` for `config` and `datamodule` to avoid potential attribute errors during `class_names` sync.

Apply class, method, and field renames to Java codebase

Phase 1: Renamed 88 classes (e.g., hc→Logger, aH→AppSettings, eY→JsonObject)
- Replaced all type references, imports, and declarations using word boundaries
- Sorted longest names first to avoid partial match conflicts

Phase 2: Renamed 89 .java source files to match new class names

Phase 3: Renamed 38 methods including:
- Application: getInstance, formatDateTime, loadIcon, loadScaledIcon, loadSave, etc.
- SaveLocation: detectSaveLocation, createSaveLocation, getStorageName
- fromPlayerData on Exosuit, Multitool, Starship, SquadronPilot, etc.

Phase 4: Renamed 43 Application fields (instance, iconCache, mainWindow, etc.)

Special handling:
- Overloaded methods disambiguated by parameter signature and arg count
- Class rename collisions with method names resolved (e.g., a→AboutDialog affected
  methods named 'a' which were subsequently correctly renamed to their target names)
- Inner class accessor methods distinguished from public API methods
- Field Application.aH and class aH handled separately

commit.message:
Replace custom Vale workflow with vale-cli/vale-action (#2062)

The Vale CI workflow was ~320 lines of hand-rolled shell that manually
installed Vale, deleted old PR comments, ran Vale, parsed JSON output,
and posted inline review comments one-by-one. All of this is handled
natively by `vale-cli/vale-action` (backed by reviewdog).

## What changed

- **Replaced** manual Vale install + comment management + raw GitHub API
comment posting with `vale-cli/vale-action@v2.1.1`
- **Kept** Node.js + pnpm install and `PATH` setup — required for
`mdx2vast`, the MDX parser Vale needs
- **Kept** `git diff`-based changed-file detection with all existing
exclusions (`snippets/`, `errors/`, `.skills/`, `agent/cli-api.mdx`,
`agent/cli.mdx`, `style-guide.mdx`)

## On the old "comment cleanup" logic

The delete-before-post loop existed because the raw comment-posting
approach had no deduplication — identical comments would pile up on each
push. reviewdog natively deduplicates by checking existing review
comments before posting, and GitHub marks stale comments "outdated"
automatically when code changes. No manual cleanup needed.

## Key settings

- `reporter: github-pr-review` — inline PR review comments, same as
before
- `filter_mode: nofilter` — report all issues in changed files, not just
newly added lines (necessary because Vale line numbers can diverge from
diff positions after MDX parsing)
- `fail_on_error: false` — non-blocking

Note: vale-action v2.1.1 internally passes `-level=info` to reviewdog
when `fail_on_error: false`, so both errors and warnings are reported
regardless of the `level` input.
